mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes real corporate earnings and increases discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If walk-forward average forward return in the top quartile of CPIAUCSL levels is positive, or if rank IC over any rolling 2-year window is above 0.03, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above the 4.0% threshold, it signals deteriorating labor demand and weakening consumer income, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the median forward 20-day return following UNRATE>4.0% triggers is not negative, or less negative than baseline, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates overheating economy that forces aggressive monetary tightening, depressing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint trigger produces positive median forward returns or the interaction term in a regression has p-value > 0.10 over rolling 3-year windows, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS rising for at least 3 months), consecutive declines in housing starts (HOUST) signal a pending economic slowdown and credit contraction, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances during tightening cycles, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO >0.5% month-over-month) is a leading indicator of manufacturing contraction and weakening economic activity, yielding negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average forward 5-day return following the trigger is not negative, or if the strategy Sharpe ratio over any 3-year rolling period is above 0, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales remain robust (RSAFS > 660k) while the unemployment rate is extremely low (UNRATE < 4.0%), the economy is overheating. This combination forces the Fed to tighten, leading to negative market returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If the triggered forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases over any 3-year walk-forward, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflects strong consumer demand and supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days on an always-on basis.
failure_condition: If the correlation between RSAFS growth and forward 20-day returns is negative over any 5-year rolling window, or the rank IC is below -0.03, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A decisive increase in the federal funds rate above the 1.0% threshold after an extended near-zero period marks the beginning of monetary tightening that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.0% after being below 0.5% for at least 6 months
failure_condition: If the median forward 60-day return following the trigger is positive, or if the drawdown-adjusted return is greater than zero over any 5-year period, the hypothesis is falsified.
